D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

ICtCp — Encoding HDR Light

slide a colour up the PQ curve and watch the intensity channel rise non-linearly while Ct and Cp carry the chroma

BT.2100 ICtCp encodes HDR by passing the cone responses through the PQ curve, then splitting into intensity (I) and two chroma axes (Ct, Cp). PQ spends most of its code values on the dark end, where vision is most sensitive — so I climbs steeply at low luminance.

Set the luminance and hue of a Rec.2020 colour and the display's peak L_p. The top panel marks where the colour sits on the PQ curve; the lower plot places it on the Ct–Cp chroma plane. ITP simply halves Ct so ΔE distances are uniform.
